content.problemStatement:
A particular month has 303030 days, numbered from 111 to 303030.

Day 111 is a Monday, and the usual 777-day week is followed (so day 222 is Tuesday, day 333 is Wednesday, and so on).

Every Saturday and Sunday is a holiday. There are NNN festival days, which are also holidays. Note that it is possible for a festival day to occur on a Saturday or Sunday.

You are given the dates of the festivals. Determine the total number of holidays in this month.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN denoting the number of festival days.
The second line of each test case contains NNN **distinct** space-separated integers A1,A2,…ANA_1, A_2, \ldots A_NA1​,A2​,…AN​, denoting the festival days. Note that the AiA_iAi​ are **not necessarily given in sorted order.**


Output Format
For each test case, output a new line containing the total number of holidays.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases.
For each test case:
- The first line contains an integer N, the number of festival days.
- The second line contains N distinct integers A1 A2 … AN, the dates of the festivals (1 ≤ Ai ≤ 30).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the total number of holidays in the month.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Weekends are days 6,7,13,14,20,21,27,28.
Test case 1: festivals {5,7}. Day 7 is already a weekend, so holidays = 8 weekends + day 5 = 9.
Test case 2: festivals {23,1,6}. Day 6 is a weekend, so add days 1 and 23 to the 8 weekends → 10.
Test case 3: festival {13} is already a weekend, so total holidays remain 8.

hidden.label: edge case – festivals only on weekends

solution.approach: The month has exactly 30 days. Saturdays are days 6,13,20,27 and Sundays are days 7,14,21,28. These eight days are always holidays. For each test case we create a boolean array of size 31 (index 1..30) and mark the eight weekend days as true. Then we read the N festival days and mark each of them as true as well. Finally we count how many indices are true – that is the number of distinct holidays (weekends plus festivals, without double‑counting). The algorithm is O(30 + N) per test case, which is O(1) because the limits are tiny.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * (30 + N)) → O(T) because 30 and N ≤ 30 are constants.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(30) → O(1) extra space per test case.
